similar_questions.title: Find Resultant Array After Removing Anagrams

discussion_posts.name: JensHandsomeUwe
discussion_posts.content: Just wanna give props to myself. This was my first medium :)

discussion_posts.name: exemplles
discussion_posts.content:
`
An Anagram is a word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of a different word or phrase, typically using all the original letters exactly once.`
Input:
`strs = ["",""]`
Expected:
`[["",""]]`

Where are the letters or words?

discussion_posts.name: SawanKushwah
discussion_posts.content:
Approach 
take out string 
char array 
sort it 
convert to string 
use this string as a key 
then push all the related element to key

discussion_posts.name: htzfun
discussion_posts.content: What does it mean "return all groups"? But the return result is vector<string>? How can we return all groups? I mean, for example, we have such vector ["dog","cat","god","tac"]. What should I return?

discussion_posts.name: MrLebovsky
discussion_posts.content:
It says: " You can return the answer in any order", but console result is:

Output
[["ddddddddddg","dgggggggggg"]]
Expected
[["dgggggggggg"],["ddddddddddg"]]

Why?

discussion_posts.name: anwendeng
discussion_posts.content:
Hash table must be used, because this is a strings' question.
One tip:  strings are anagrams <=> same string after sorting. No need for frequency count unless the string is super long.
Have a nice day!

discussion_posts.name: mahdy
discussion_posts.content: Why the output is list and not a list of list. What If there are multiple groups of anagrams?
